idea-plugin.name: Stack Doctor
idea-plugin.description:
Stack Doctor — Docker Compose service map & network doctor

Turns a docker-compose.yml into a visual service dependency map and runs a set of "doctor" checks for the networking mistakes that waste hours:

  • Service dependency graph (depends_on / shared networks)
  • Ports published to 127.0.0.1 that won't be reachable from other devices
  • Missing healthchecks on services other containers depend on
  • Suspicious / non-existent host volume paths
  • depends_on pointing at services that don't exist, and dependency cycles
  • Missing env_file references
  • Ports that are exposed internally vs actually published

Getting started

  1. Install & open a project. Stack Doctor works in any JetBrains IDE (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, Rider, and more). Open a project that contains at least one Compose file — anything named like docker-compose.yml, compose.yaml, or a *.yml/*.yaml file with compose in its name. They're discovered automatically; there's nothing to configure.
  2. Open the tool window. Go to View | Tool Windows | Stack Doctor (it docks on the right). You'll see every Compose file as a root node, with its services branching to the right and depends_on arrows showing what starts before what.
  3. Read the diagnosis. Findings are listed below the map and highlighted inline in the YAML editor. Nodes tint amber or red when a service — or a whole file — has warnings or errors, so problem areas stand out at a glance.
  4. Drill in. Click any node for a line-by-line breakdown of its configuration and issues; double-click to jump straight to it in the editor. Not sure what a symbol, line, or colour means? Hit Legend in the toolbar.
  5. Fix it. Apply the inline quick-fixes on highlighted YAML (bind a port to all interfaces, declare a missing volume or network, and more), or click Add healthchecks to generate context-aware healthchecks for the services that need one.

Press the Refresh button in the tool window anytime to re-scan the project and re-run every check.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.3.0

  • Merged preview. A new Merged preview button combines any two compose files into one — pick a base (defaults to docker-compose.yml) and an overlay (e.g. docker-compose.override.yml) and see the effective configuration Docker would run, with the doctor checks re-run on the merged result and overlay-contributed values highlighted.
  • Collapsible warnings & errors. The diagnostics list at the bottom now has a header you can click to collapse it out of the way and expand it again.
  • Draggable nodes. Drag service and file nodes to rearrange the map; the dependency arrows follow, and your layout is kept until you press Reset layout (or restart the IDE).
  • Add fields from the service form. The edit form now has an Add field button to add parameters a service doesn't have yet — healthcheck, restart policy, expose, and more — then fill them in and Save. Long field hints now wrap instead of being clipped at the popup edge.

0.2.1

  • The service edit form now includes an editable Healthcheck field, so a generated healthcheck can be tweaked in place.
  • Fixed edit-form fields being clipped on the right edge — they now fit the popup and resize with it.

0.2.0

  • Edit services right from the map. Click a service node to open an editable form for its parameters — image, ports, expose, depends_on, volumes, networks, env files, environment and restart — and Save writes the changes straight back into the compose file in one undoable step.
  • The map legend now describes what each service category is for, instead of repeating the same note.
  • Updated for the latest JetBrains IDEs; now supports 2025.3 and newer.

0.1.1

  • New plugin logo and tool-window icon.
  • Fixed the map background flashing over a custom IDE background image — the map now overlays the IDE background directly instead of painting a solid fill behind the nodes.
  • Added a "Getting started" guide to the plugin description.

0.1.0

  • Initial release: whole-project Compose service map with dependency layout.
  • Ten networking "doctor" checks, surfaced inline and in the tool window.
  • Context-aware healthcheck generation (bulk, per-service, and as a quick-fix).
  • Map colours now follow the active IDE theme.

action.description: Re-scan the project for Compose files and re-run diagnostics.
